verses.text: 'To every thing there is a season': a time to be born and to die, to plant and pluck up, to weep and to laugh. Life moves in appointed seasons. God orders the times, and wisdom bows to His ordering rather than fighting it.

'To every thing there is a season': a time to be born and to die, to plant and pluck up, to weep and to laugh. Life moves in appointed seasons. God orders the times, and wisdom bows to His ordering rather than fighting it.

'To every thing there is a season': a time to be born and to die, to plant and pluck up, to weep and to laugh. Life moves in appointed seasons. God orders the times, and wisdom bows to His ordering rather than fighting it.

'To every thing there is a season': a time to be born and to die, to plant and pluck up, to weep and to laugh. Life moves in appointed seasons. God orders the times, and wisdom bows to His ordering rather than fighting it.
So too a time to embrace and refrain, to keep and cast away, to love and hate, war and peace. Every season has its turn. The believer learns to act fittingly for the time God has set.
So too a time to embrace and refrain, to keep and cast away, to love and hate, war and peace. Every season has its turn. The believer learns to act fittingly for the time God has set.

So too a time to embrace and refrain, to keep and cast away, to love and hate, war and peace. Every season has its turn. The believer learns to act fittingly for the time God has set.

What profit is toil? Yet 'he hath made every thing beautiful in his time,' and set eternity in man's heart, though none can fully trace His work. We sense more than we can grasp. God plants a longing for the everlasting that earth cannot satisfy.

There is no better thing than to rejoice and do good, to eat and enjoy the fruit of labour, 'for it is the gift of God.' Contentment is His gift. The wise receive each day's good as grace, not as their own achievement.

There is no better thing than to rejoice and do good, to eat and enjoy the fruit of labour, 'for it is the gift of God.' Contentment is His gift. The wise receive each day's good as grace, not as their own achievement.
Whatever God does endures for ever, nothing added or taken, 'that men should fear before him.' His works are unchangeable. The permanence of God's doings is meant to stir reverence in fleeting man.
Whatever God does endures for ever, nothing added or taken, 'that men should fear before him.' His works are unchangeable. The permanence of God's doings is meant to stir reverence in fleeting man.

Seeing injustice even where judgment should be, Solomon trusts that 'God shall judge the righteous and the wicked.' Wrong is not the last word. The crookedness of earth will be set right at God's appointed judgment.

Man and beast alike die and return to dust, and who fully knows the spirit's path? So let a man rejoice in his works. Death humbles human pride. Yet faith holds that the spirit of man, unlike the beast, returns to God.
